title: Rail-Track Laying and Maintenance Equipment Operators
description: Lay, repair, and maintain track for standard or narrow-gauge railroad equipment used in regular railroad service or in plant yards, quarries, sand and gravel pits, and mines. Includes ballast cleaning machine operators and railroad bed tamping machine operators.

tasks.text: Patrol assigned track sections so that damaged or broken track can be located and reported.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair or adjust track switches, using wrenches and replacement parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Weld sections of track together, such as switch points and frogs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe leveling indicator arms to verify levelness and alignment of tracks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate single- or multiple-head spike driving machines to drive spikes into ties and secure rails.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate track wrenches to tighten or loosen bolts at joints that hold ends of rails together.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut rails to specified lengths, using rail saws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lubricate machines, change oil, or fill hydraulic reservoirs to specified levels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drill holes through rails, tie plates, or fishplates for insertion of bolts or spikes, using power drills.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean tracks or clear ice or snow from tracks or switch boxes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, grade, or level ballast on railroad tracks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Raise rails, using hydraulic jacks, to allow for tie removal and replacement.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust controls of machines that spread, shape, raise, level, or align track, according to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dress and reshape worn or damaged railroad switch points or frogs, using portable power grinders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean or make minor repairs to machines or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Grind ends of new or worn rails to attain smooth joints, using portable grinders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate single- or multiple-head spike pullers to pull old spikes from ties.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: String and attach wire-guidelines machine to rails so that tracks or rails can be aligned or leveled.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Engage mechanisms that lay tracks or rails to specified gauges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive graders, tamping machines, brooms, or ballast spreading machines to redistribute gravel or ballast between rails.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive vehicles that automatically move and lay tracks or rails over sections of track to be constructed, repaired, or maintained.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn wheels of machines, using lever controls, to adjust guidelines for track alignments or grades, following specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Push controls to close grasping devices on track or rail sections so that they can be raised or moved.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate tie-adzing machines to cut ties and permit insertion of fishplates that hold rails.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Paint railroad signs, such as speed limits or gate-crossing warnings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Spray ties, fishplates, or joints with oil to protect them from weathering.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
